app.title: PTCG Probability Lab
app.tagline: A measuring instrument for deck probability
locale.label: Language
view.comingSoon: This workspace arrives in a later phase.
view.ask.title: Ask
view.ask.q1.title: Q1 — Basic Pokémon in the opening 7
view.ask.q2coming: The Q2 sentence query builder arrives in Phase 3.
q2.title: Q2 — Opening combo query
q2.empty: Pick at least one card from your deck with "+ and".
q2.headline.label: Event probability
q2.headline.interpret: In real games, roughly 1 in {games} openings achieves this.
q2.headline.interpretRaw: Roughly 1 in {games} openings achieves this (no mulligan correction).
q2.table.caption: Joint distribution table
q2.table.satisfiedLegend: Blue left edge = combinations satisfying every constraint
q3.title: Q3 — Cards in the prize pile
q3.h.label: in hand
q3.ob.label: other Basics
q3.mode.uncond.info: ⓘ Before seeing any card: each copy is prized with exactly 1/10; E = x/10.
q3.mode.givenHand.info: ⓘ You hold h copies; the remaining x−h sit among 53 unseen cards, 6 of which are prizes.
q3.mode.preGame.info: ⓘ Conditioned on a valid opening hand, marginalized over it — the direction flips with whether the card is a Basic Pokémon.
q3.preGame.impossible: This setup can never produce a valid hand (no Basic Pokémon at all) — adjust the Basic flag or the other-Basics count.
q3.headline.label: At least 1 copy prized
q3.headline.interpret: Roughly 1 in {games} games, at least 1 copy of this card is prized.
q3.direction.below: E sits below the baseline: a valid hand tends to grab extra Basics, leaving fewer for the prizes.
q3.direction.above: E sits above the baseline: a valid hand claims at least one Basic slot, so non-Basics stay in the deck slightly more often.
q3.direction.equal: E equals the unconditional baseline x/10.
q3.joint.title: Multi-card joint (known hand)
q3.joint.desc: Each tracked card has x−h copies among the 53 unseen cards; the joint distribution over the 6 prizes with [min,max] constraints.
q3.joint.empty: Add at least one card to compute the joint prized probability.
q3.joint.label: Card name (optional)
q3.joint.headline: Joint event probability
preset.title: Preset ten questions
preset.jointDemo: A4 h1 + B3 h0, each ≥1 prized?
curve.title: Turn curve — ≥w copies seen by turn T
curve.honesty: No mulligan correction in this curve (02 §6.3); under current rules the first player DOES draw on turn 1 — the historical rule is an opt-in toggle.
curve.legacyRule: Historical rule: first player skips the first draw
grade.title: Opening-hand grading (mulligan-aware)
grade.desc: Define ideal and playable hands via at-least-n constraints; the rest is dead. All three grades are conditioned on a valid hand.
grade.needDefs: Add at least one card to the ideal or playable definition first.
grade.attribution.title: Dead-hand attribution (±1 copy)
grade.attribution.assumption: Each card's count is perturbed ±1 and the dead rate recomputed; deck size held constant via generic non-Basic filler. Negative (green) = dead rate drops.
compare.title: A/B deck comparison
compare.notComputable: One of the decks cannot be computed (under 7 cards, or the mulligan correction needs Basic Pokémon).
trainer.title: Probability intuition training
trainer.desc: The only tool you may carry into a tournament is your intuition. Questions use your deck's real numbers — guess, then check the exact value.
trainer.history.title: Error history
trainer.q.mulligan: Your deck runs {b} Basic Pokémon out of {n}. The probability of opening with a mulligan is?
trainer.q.openingAtLeast1: {name} ({x} copies): in the real game (mulligan-aware), the probability of opening with at least 1 is?
trainer.q.prizedAtLeast1: {name} ({x} copies): the pre-game probability that at least 1 copy is prized is?
trainer.q.seenByTurn: {name} ({x} copies): going second, by your turn {t} ({n} cards seen), the probability of having seen at least 1 is?
tracker.title: In-game prize tracker
tracker.desc: Tick off every card you have SEEN (opening hand, draws, searches…); the posterior probability that each key card is prized updates exactly.
tracker.tooManySeen: Seen count exceeds the physical cap (54 max) — check the inputs.
share.tooLong: The link exceeds 2KB — consider sharing the exported deck list instead.
share.error: This share link could not be parsed — it may be corrupted or from an incompatible version.
sens.title: Sensitivity: what is copy #{count} of "{name}" worth?
sens.assumption: Counts 0–4 swept (deck held at 60 via generic non-Basic filler); the current query's lower bound applies.
mc.note: Simulations jitter; the exact value does not.
mc.outside: (rarely outside the band — run it again and see)
mc.band.aria: Simulation confidence band vs the exact value
energy.title: Energy shortfall curve (02 §6.4 · golden pipeline v2)
energy.desc: Probability of having seen fewer than w energy by your turn T — conditioned on a valid opening hand (mulligan-aware).
energy.guard: Needs a 60-card deck with at least 1 Basic Pokémon; energy + Basics may not exceed 60.
luck.title: Luck meter (02 §10)
luck.desc: How surprising is n straight games without the key event? Games are independent; the tail is (1−p)^n, exact throughout.
luck.verdict.rare: Tail < 5% — this streak is genuinely rare; you really were unlucky.
luck.verdict.normal: Tail ≥ 5% — this streak is unremarkable; no need to blame luck yet.
luck.deckNote: Luck only measures surprise; for deck problems, check the grading view's dead rate and attribution.
museum.title: Fallacy museum (02 §9)
museum.desc: Four popular wrong methods vs the exact value — even the wrong numbers are computed exactly, so the error is unmistakable.
museum.d1.title: Independence fallacy (A4+B4, each ≥1)
museum.d1.lesson: Cards in one hand compete for slots — counts are negatively correlated. Multiplying overestimates by ~1.4pp.
museum.d2.title: Binomial (with-replacement) approximation (4-of, ≥1)
museum.d2.lesson: A 60-card deck draws without replacement — the approximation underestimates by ~1.6pp.
museum.d3.title: Ignoring mulligans (A4 Basic + B3, ob6)
museum.d3.lesson: The real game only deals you valid hands — ignoring mulligans systematically underestimates by ~4pp. This is why this tool exists.
museum.d4.title: "6 prizes × 1/10 = 40%" (4-of prized)
museum.d4.lesson: Expectations add (E = 0.4 is right); probabilities do not — P(≥1 prized) is 35.15%, not 40%.
basiclist.title: Import a Basic-Pokémon list (community JSON)
basiclist.desc: Paste a name → isBasic JSON (plain card names; bilingual lists welcome). It merges into the global memory and retags matching cards in every deck.
basiclist.error: Could not parse the JSON — the shape must be {"card name": true/false}.
tools.title: Deck tools
fold.title: Search-chain fold (02 §4.3 · golden pipeline v2)
fold.desc: Fold searchers that fetch the target into effective copies — optimistic and conservative side by side; the truth lies between. Mulligan-aware.
fold.gap: Gap {gap} — the upper bound on what the search chain is worth.
fold.guard: Cannot compute: the target must be in the deck, and the mulligan correction needs at least 1 Basic Pokémon.
fold.assume.1: Assumption 1: every searcher succeeds (no fail case, no disruption).
fold.assume.2: Assumption 2: a target copy remains in the deck when searching.
fold.assume.3: Assumption 3: searchers are not Basic Pokémon and never make a hand valid.
fold.assume.4: Assumption 4: conservative = no search at all; real games land between the two.
opt.title: Local deck optimizer (02 §11 · golden pipeline v2)
opt.desc: Lock the rest of the deck, spread free slots over up to 3 candidates (remainder to generic non-Basic filler), and enumerate every allocation exactly.
opt.assumption: Objective = every candidate opens with ≥ its required copies (mulligan-aware); leftover slots become generic non-Basic filler.
relay.title: Multi-turn relay event (02 §6.5 · golden pipeline v2)
relay.desc: "Seen A by turn T_A AND B by turn T_B" — the real assembly rate of a combo script. No mulligan correction.
alias.title: Card-name aliases (D1)
alias.desc: Point an alias (e.g. an English card name) at its canonical name — Basic-Pokémon tags are shared globally through the map (import wizard, rename autofill).
bank.save.placeholder: Shortcut name (saves the current Q3 query)
q1.headline.label: Mulligan probability
q1.headline.interpret: Roughly 1 in {games} games opens with a mulligan; each one hands your opponent an extra card.
q1.headline.neverMulligan: This deck can never mulligan — fewer than 7 non-Basic cards.
q1.dist.title: Opening distribution
q1.dist.toggleAria: Choose the conditioning of the distribution
q1.conditional.note: The k = 0 row is always 0 — the real game never deals you a hand without a Basic Pokémon.
q1.chart.aria: Bar chart of Basic Pokémon in the opening hand ({mode})
view.prizes.title: Prizes
view.prizes.desc: Probability of cards being prized (Q3) — Phase 4.
view.compare.title: Compare
view.compare.desc: A/B deck comparison with deltas — Phase 5.

empty.deck: No deck yet. Paste a PTCG Live list, or start with your first card.
error.deckCount: Currently {n}/60 cards — still computable, but results describe an {n}-card deck.
error.basicUnknown: Mark which cards are Basic Pokémon first to enable the mulligan correction.
receipt.footer: Exact BigInt fractions throughout; decimals shown round-half-up.
tracker.legal: Using external tools during sanctioned play may violate tournament rules; this feature is for practice and review.
q1.conditional.title: The hand you actually get (mulligan-aware)
deck.list.title: My decks
deck.delete.confirm: Delete deck "{name}"? This cannot be undone.
deck.card.name: Card name
deck.count.label: Total cards
summary.title: Deck summary
summary.noBasics: No Basic Pokémon marked yet — mulligan probability cannot be computed.
summary.needCards: The deck needs at least 7 cards to compute opening-hand probabilities.
import.title: Import a PTCG Live list
import.step1.title: Step 1 / 2: paste the list
import.step1.placeholder: Paste the deck list text exported from PTCG Live…
import.step1.empty: Paste the deck list text first.
import.warn.notSixty: Total {n} ≠ 60: import is still allowed for decks under construction.
import.warn.unparsed: {n} line(s) could not be parsed and were skipped.
import.step2.title: Step 2 / 2: mark Basic Pokémon
import.step2.desc: A text list cannot tell which cards are Basic Pokémon. Mark the Pokémon section line by line — this is a required input for the mulligan math, remembered globally by card name.
import.step2.nonPokemon: Non-Pokémon sections ({n} cards) need no marking.
export.title: Export list (PTCG Live format)
trial.title: Trial table (honest dealer)
trial.desc: Seeded dealing under the real rules: 7-card hand, mulligan loop, 6 prize cards and the first draw. The exact probabilities always sit beside the sample stats — simulation builds intuition, the exact value is the answer.
trial.empty: Press “Deal a hand” to start; the same seed reproduces the same sequence.
trial.tooFew: Dealing needs at least {n} cards (7 hand + 6 prizes + 1 draw).
trial.notSixty: This deck has {n} cards (not 60) — dealing still works; exact values use the real count.
trial.noBasics: No card is tagged as a Basic Pokémon: a real game would mulligan forever. The mulligan loop is disabled here and the deal is shown unconditioned.
trial.prizes: Prize cards ×6 (hidden in a real game; revealed here for practice)
trial.teach: Every deal is one Bernoulli(q) trial; samples always carry error, the exact value never does. That is the difference between sampling and computing.
